summary: Iteration 2 RETEST complete — full Playwright runtime verification of the new device card on the Biomarkers tab passed end-to-end, plus full regression on Today / Trends / Invie / Vault, plus 18/18 backend pytest. Device card flow verified at runtime: device-card renders above biomarker-latest (y=161 vs y=472), PNG asset loads (820×440 natural size), initial LED='OFFLINE' with Connect button & disabled Run, clicking Run while disabled does nothing (LED stays OFFLINE), Connect → LED='PAIRING' / btn='Pairing…' within 500 ms → LED='READY' / btn='Connected' after ~1.8 s, Run then enabled and produces LED='PROCESSING' with progress bar and 'Running 19%' label cycling, after ~4.2 s LED returns to 'READY' with btn='Run' and 'LAST RUN HH:MM' appears at top-right of card, exactly one fresh GET /api/biomarkers/siga fires on completion. Chip switching (cortisol → testosterone → creatinine → siga) keeps the device-card visible and retains 'Connected' state. Today, Trends (2w/6w/3m), Invie chat (sIgA reply 'Your latest sIgA reading is 174 µg/mL …'), and Vault Export ('Export queued — RGPD machine-readable JSON …') all still work. No red screens. One harmless web-only warning observed: 'Animated: useNativeDriver is not supported … falling back to JS-based animation' — expected on Expo web preview, no action needed.
critical_code_review_comments: biomarkers.tsx is now 600 lines and approaching the 700-line soft cap — consider extracting <DeviceCard/> (lines 144–265) into /src/components/DeviceCard.tsx with its own bleState/run logic for testability and reuse.

onRun() relies on setInterval with Date.now() which is fine on web but will keep firing if the screen unmounts mid-run — wrap the interval id in a ref and clear it in a useEffect cleanup to be safe on real native navigation.

Bluetooth simulation is a frontend setTimeout/setInterval mock as flagged in mocked_api — when integrating real BLE (expo-bluetooth/react-native-ble-plx) keep the same testIDs (device-connect, device-run) so this Playwright suite still applies.
seed_data_creation: None — backend auto-seeds the Léa Moreau demo dataset on startup. Onboarding gate bypassed via localStorage.setItem('labs.onboarded','1').
context_for_next_testing_agent: Device card testIDs: device-card (wrapper), device-connect (Bluetooth button), device-run (Run button). LED state text is one of OFFLINE / PAIRING / READY / PROCESSING and lives inside the device-card. Connect simulation is ~1.6s (setTimeout in biomarkers.tsx:76), Run simulation is ~4.2s (setInterval in biomarkers.tsx:84). 'last run' label uses uppercase typography so querying for the lowercase string fails — assert via screenshot or check uppercase 'LAST RUN'. Onboarding bypass: localStorage key 'labs.onboarded' = '1'. Bottom-tab labels remain uppercase TODAY/BIOMARKERS/TRENDS/INVIE/VAULT. Trends segment labels are '2 weeks' / '6 weeks' / '3 months' (NOT '2w/6w/3m'). Invie chat input is testID invie-input, send button invie-send.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no functional bugs found. The previous iteration's static-only review is now backed by runtime Playwright verification on a 375×812 mobile viewport against the public preview URL.
